title: DRDO CEPTAM-11 Memory Based Held on (12 Nov 2022) #DRDOQS001.json

questions.text: Which of the following statements is INCORRECT?
options: There is a repulsive force when north poles (or south poles) of two magnets are brought close together.
If a bar magnet is broken into two halves, we get two similar bar magnets with stronger properties.
The earth behaves as a magnet with the magnetic field pointing approximately from the geographic south to the north.
When a bar magnet is freely suspended, it points in the north-south direction.
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: B** (If a bar magnet is broken into two halves, we get two similar bar magnets with stronger properties.)

**Detailed Explanation:**
When a bar magnet is broken into two halves, each piece becomes an independent magnet with its own north and south poles. The magnetic strength of each new magnet is **not stronger** than the original; in fact, the pole strength remains the same or slightly weaker due to division of magnetic moment. This is a fundamental property of magnets and is tested in DRDO exams because electromagnetic devices (missiles, radars, sensors) rely on precise understanding of magnetic domains.

**Why other options are correct/incorrect:**
• **A is correct** – Like poles repel (fundamental law of magnetism; used in magnetic levitation systems in defence).
• **C is correct** – Earth’s magnetic field points from geographic South to North (magnetic South pole lies near geographic North; critical for navigation in defence applications).
• **D is correct** – Freely suspended magnet aligns with Earth’s magnetic field (basis of magnetic compass used in military navigation).

**DRDO Relevance:** Understanding magnet breaking is essential for designing permanent magnets in motors, generators and guidance systems.

questions.text: Which of the following is the number of oscillations per unit time of the sound wave?
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: D** (Frequency)

**Detailed Explanation:**
Frequency is defined as the number of oscillations/vibrations per unit time of a sound wave. It is measured in Hertz (Hz). Higher frequency means higher pitch. This concept is vital in DRDO for sonar, acoustic sensors and communication systems.

**Option-wise Analysis:**
• **A (Wavelength)** – Distance between two consecutive points in same phase; inversely proportional to frequency (λ = v/f). Incorrect.
• **B (Time period)** – Time for one complete oscillation (T = 1/f). Incorrect.
• **C (Amplitude)** – Maximum displacement; determines loudness, not frequency. Incorrect.

**DRDO Context:** Frequency is used in ultrasonic testing of defence materials and underwater acoustics.

questions.text: The tissue providing nutrition to the embryo in the uterus is the ______.
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: C** (Placenta)

**Detailed Explanation:**
The placenta is a temporary endocrine organ that connects the fetus to the uterine wall, enabling nutrient, gas and waste exchange via diffusion. It secretes hCG, estrogen and progesterone. Formed after implantation.

**Option-wise Analysis:**
• **A & B (Fallopian tube/Oviduct)** – Site of fertilization; no nutrition role.
• **D (Villi)** – Chorionic villi are part of placenta but alone do not provide full nutrition.

**DRDO Relevance:** Knowledge of human reproduction is tested for medical research and biotechnology posts.

questions.text: ______ compound is used as a drying agent.
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: A** (Calcium oxide)

**Detailed Explanation:**
Calcium oxide (quicklime, CaO) reacts exothermically with water (CaO + H₂O → Ca(OH)₂) and absorbs moisture efficiently. Used in labs and industry.

**Option-wise Analysis:**
• **B** – Produces acetylene gas.
• **C** – Contains water of crystallization; used in cement.
• **D** – Not hygroscopic.

**DRDO Context:** Drying agents are crucial in chemical defence labs and propellant manufacturing.

questions.text: If the radius of curvature of a concave mirror is 6.2 cm, its focal length is ______ cm.
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: B** (3.1)

**Detailed Explanation:**
For spherical mirrors, focal length f = R/2.
Given R = 6.2 cm, f = 6.2 / 2 = **3.1 cm**.

**Option-wise Analysis:**
• A is 2R (wrong).
• C & D do not follow the formula.

**Formula (KaTeX):** $$f = \frac{R}{2}$$
**DRDO Relevance:** Concave mirrors are used in solar concentrators, headlights and optical instruments in defence.

questions.text:
Consider the following statements:
1. Saturated hydrocarbons contain single bonds only.
2. Ethyne is an example of a saturated hydrocarbon.
3. Carbon can form long chain compounds due to catenation.

Which are correct?
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: B** (1 and 3 only)

**Detailed Explanation:**
Statement 1 & 3 are correct. Saturated hydrocarbons (alkanes) have only single bonds (CₙH₂ₙ₊₂). Carbon shows catenation due to strong C-C bonds.
Statement 2 is wrong – Ethyne (C₂H₂) is unsaturated (triple bond, alkyne).

**Option-wise Analysis:** All other combinations include wrong statement 2.

**DRDO Relevance:** Hydrocarbons are studied for fuels and polymers in defence materials.

questions.text: ______ compound has –OH as a functional group.
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: D** (Alcohol)

**Detailed Explanation:**
Alcohols have –OH (hydroxyl) group bonded to carbon (R-OH). Example: Ethanol.

**Option-wise Analysis:**
• A, B, C all have –COOH group.

**DRDO Relevance:** Alcohols are used as fuels and solvents in defence labs.

questions.text: A car of mass m accelerates uniformly from rest under a constant force F. After traveling a distance d, what is the kinetic energy of the car?
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: B** (F × d)

**Detailed Explanation:**
By Work-Energy Theorem: Work done = Change in KE.
Initial KE = 0 → Final KE = W = F × d (θ = 0°).

**Formula (KaTeX):** $$W = F \times d \times \cos 0^\circ = F \times d$$

**Option-wise Analysis:**
• A is wrong (no 1/2 factor here).
• C & D violate the theorem.

**DRDO Relevance:** Applied in vehicle dynamics and projectile motion studies.

questions.text: 'Alum' is an example of:
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: D** (Double salt)

**Detailed Explanation:**
Alum (K₂SO₄·Al₂(SO₄)₃·24H₂O) is a double salt (potash alum).

**Option-wise Analysis:** Others are single salts or forms of NaCl.

**DRDO Relevance:** Used in water purification for defence camps.

questions.text: Which statement is NOT true for magnetic field lines?
options: The relative strength of the magnetic field is shown by the degree of closeness of the field lines.
No two field lines are found to cross each other.
Inside the magnet, the direction of field lines is from its south pole to its north pole.
Magnetic field lines are not closed curves.
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: D** (Magnetic field lines are not closed curves.)

**Detailed Explanation:**
Magnetic field lines are **always closed curves** (outside N to S, inside S to N).

**Option-wise Analysis:** A, B, C are true.

**DRDO Relevance:** Used in designing electromagnetic shields and MRI in defence medical tech.

questions.text: If a person moves a trolley for a distance of 10 m with a force of 50 N, then the work done is:
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: D** (500 J)

**Detailed Explanation:**
W = F × d × cos0° = 50 × 10 = **500 J**.

**DRDO Relevance:** Basic work-energy concept for mechanical systems.

questions.text: What was the unknown element under aluminium called?
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: C** (Eka-Aluminium)

**Detailed Explanation:**
Mendeleev predicted Gallium as Eka-Aluminium (below Al in periodic table).

**DRDO Relevance:** Periodic table knowledge is foundational for materials science in defence alloys.

questions.text: Which of the following laboratory safety equipments is used to extinguish small fires?
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: D** (Fire extinguisher)

**Detailed Explanation:**
CO₂ or dry powder extinguishers are standard in labs.

**Option-wise Analysis:** Others are not for fire extinguishing.

**DRDO Relevance:** Lab safety is mandatory in chemical defence research.

questions.text:
Which of the following is an example of animal hormones?
I. Adrenalin
II. Testosterone
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: D** (Both I and II)

**Detailed Explanation:**
Adrenalin (fight-or-flight) and Testosterone (male sex hormone) are animal hormones.

**DRDO Relevance:** Endocrinology knowledge for bio-defence and medical research.

questions.text: Which of the following has disinfectant properties? A. Amphoteric surfactant B. Evaporative cleaner C. Cationic detergent
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: A** (Only C)

**Detailed Explanation:**
Cationic detergents (quats) destroy microbial membranes.

**DRDO Relevance:** Used in sterilization of defence equipment.

questions.text: Which of the following modern physicist closely examined Newtonian mechanics' foundations?
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: D** (Albert Einstein)

**Detailed Explanation:**
Einstein’s relativity challenged Newton’s absolute space/time.

**DRDO Relevance:** Relativity is used in GPS and missile guidance.

questions.text: A sound wave with a period of 0.01 seconds has a frequency of:
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: D** (100 Hz)

**Detailed Explanation:**
f = 1/T = 1/0.01 = **100 Hz**.

**DRDO Relevance:** Sonar frequency calculations.

questions.text: Which of the following is CORRECT regarding Ohm's law?
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: B** (V ∝ I)

**Detailed Explanation:**
V = IR (constant R and temperature).

**DRDO Relevance:** Circuit design in electronics.

questions.text: ______ are those errors that tend to be in one direction, either positive or negative.
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: B** (Systematic error)

**Detailed Explanation:**
Systematic errors are consistent and do not cancel on averaging.

**DRDO Relevance:** Accuracy in precision instruments.

questions.text: In 1995, physicists Eric Cornell and Carl Wieman created the first Bose-Einstein condensate (BEC) using which isotope?
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: C** (Rubidium-87)

**Detailed Explanation:**
First BEC achieved with Rb-87 at JILA (Nobel 2001).

**DRDO Relevance:** Quantum technologies in defence.

questions.text: An alloy with ______ in it is referred to as 'amalgam'.
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: C** (mercury)

**Detailed Explanation:**
Amalgam = alloy containing mercury (e.g., dental amalgam).

**DRDO Relevance:** Alloys in metallurgy.

questions.text: The potential difference between the terminals of an electric heater is 60 V when it draws a current of 4 A from the source. What current will the heater draw if the potential difference is changed to 127.5 V?
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: A** (8.5 A)

**Detailed Explanation:**
R = 60/4 = 15 Ω → I = 127.5/15 = **8.5 A**.

**DRDO Relevance:** Electrical heating in defence equipment.

questions.text: Element X forms an oxide with the formula X₂O₃. The element X would most likely be in the same group of the periodic table as ______
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: C** (aluminium)

**Detailed Explanation:**
+3 oxidation state → Group 13 (Al forms Al₂O₃).

**DRDO Relevance:** Periodic trends for alloy design.

questions.text: What is the work done when a constant force of 20 N is applied at an angle of 30° to the direction of displacement of 13 m?
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: B** (225 Joules)

**Detailed Explanation:**
W = F d cos30° = 20 × 13 × (√3/2) ≈ **225 J**.

**Formula:** $$W = F \times d \times \cos 30^\circ$$

**DRDO Relevance:** Mechanics in weapon systems.

questions.text: The enzyme ............ present in the pancreatic juice, converts proteins to amino acids.
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: C** (Trypsin)

**Detailed Explanation:**
Trypsin (activated from trypsinogen) breaks proteins in alkaline medium.

**DRDO Relevance:** Biochemistry in bio-defence.

questions.text: What is Karst topography primarily associated with?
options: Groundwater action in limestone or dolomite regions
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: A** (Groundwater action in limestone or dolomite regions)

**Detailed Explanation:**
Karst forms by dissolution of CaCO₃ (sinkholes, caves).

**DRDO Relevance:** Geomorphology for underground defence structures.

questions.text: Which of the following inert gases is placed in period 4 of the periodic table?
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: A** (Kr)

**Detailed Explanation:**
Krypton (atomic no. 36, period 4).

**DRDO Relevance:** Noble gases in lighting and lasers.

questions.text: Plants in the ............ group are commonly called algae.
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: B** (Thallophyta)

**Detailed Explanation:**
Thallophyta (algae) – simple, aquatic, thallus body.

**DRDO Relevance:** Biofuel research.

questions.text: Calcium sulfate hemihydrate is also known as:
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: D** (Plaster of Paris)

**Detailed Explanation:**
CaSO₄·½H₂O (POP) – sets with water.

**DRDO Relevance:** Moulding and medical casts.

questions.text: A compound has an empirical formula CH₂ and a molar mass of 56 g/mol. Its molecular formula is:
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: D** (C₄H₈)

**Detailed Explanation:**
Empirical mass = 14 → n = 56/14 = 4 → (CH₂)₄ = C₄H₈.

**DRDO Relevance:** Organic chemistry in polymers.

questions.text: Which of the following correctly represents the chronological order of human development during gestation?
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: C** (Zygote → Embryo → Fetus)

**Detailed Explanation:**
Fertilization → Zygote → Embryo (8 weeks) → Fetus (till birth).

**DRDO Relevance:** Embryology in biotech.

questions.text: For the case of a curved mirror, the pole, centre of curvature and the principal focus are connected by the:
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: B** (principal axis)

**Detailed Explanation:**
All lie on the principal axis.

**DRDO Relevance:** Optics in laser weapons.

questions.text: Emperor Perumbidugu Mutharaiyar II belonged to which dynasty?
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: D** (Mutharaiyar)

**Detailed Explanation:**
Mutharaiyar dynasty (7th-9th century CE) ruled central Tamil Nadu.

**Option-wise Analysis:** Others are contemporary but different dynasties.

**DRDO Relevance:** Indian history for general awareness.

questions.text: Which of the following is correct regarding M2?
options: M2 = Currency with the public (notes and coins) + Demand deposits + Other deposits with the Reserve Bank of India.
M2 = M3 + Total deposits with Post Office Savings Organization (excluding National Savings Certificates)
M2 = M1 + Time deposits of all commercial banks and cooperative banks (excluding inter-bank time deposits)
M2 = M1 + Post Office savings deposits
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: D** (M2 = M1 + Post Office savings deposits)

**Detailed Explanation:**
M2 is narrow money + Post Office savings.

**DRDO Relevance:** Economy for defence budget awareness.

questions.text: What is the primary purpose of pedigree analysis in the context of genetics?
options: To measure the physical traits of individuals in a population
To track the inheritance of specific traits across generations within a family
To analyse the nutritional requirements of different organisms
To study the ecological interactions between species in an environment
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: B** (To track the inheritance of specific traits across generations within a family)

**Detailed Explanation:**
Pedigree charts trace genetic disorders.

**DRDO Relevance:** Genetics in bio-warfare research.

questions.text:
India is a member of which of the following groups?
1. G-20
2. BRICS
3. OECD
4. SCO
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: B** (1, 2 and 4 only)

**Detailed Explanation:**
India is member of G-20, BRICS, SCO but **not** OECD.

**DRDO Relevance:** International relations.

questions.text: Which of the following industries was NOT included in Schedule A of the Industrial Policy Resolution, 1956?
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: C** (Fertiliser)

**Detailed Explanation:**
Fertiliser was in Schedule B (joint sector).

**DRDO Relevance:** Industrial policy for self-reliance.

questions.text: Which secret society founded by Vinayak Damodar Savarkar promoted armed revolution against the British rule?
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: A** (Abhinav Bharat)

**Detailed Explanation:**
Founded 1904 in Nashik.

**DRDO Relevance:** Freedom struggle history.

questions.text: Which Article of the Indian Constitution was amended by the 106th Constitutional Amendment Act to provide for women's reservation in the Legislative Assembly of Delhi?
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: C** (Article 239AA)

**Detailed Explanation:**
New clause inserted for Delhi Assembly reservation.

**DRDO Relevance:** Polity awareness.

questions.text: Which of the following instruments is primarily used by the RBI for monetary policy implementation in the post 1991 reform era?
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: A** (Repo rate)

**Detailed Explanation:**
Repo rate under Liquidity Adjustment Facility.

**DRDO Relevance:** Economic stability for defence funding.

questions.text: Education is now placed under which list after the 42nd Amendment?
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: C** (Concurrent List)

**Detailed Explanation:**
Shifted by 42nd Amendment (1976).

**DRDO Relevance:** Education policy.

questions.text: As per the Calcutta High Court (Extension of Jurisdiction) Act, 1953, the jurisdiction of the Calcutta High Court was extended to cover which of the following areas?
options: Dadra and Nagar Haveli, and Daman and Diu
Chandannagar, and Andaman and Nicobar Islands
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: D** (Chandannagar, and Andaman and Nicobar Islands)

**Detailed Explanation:**
Extended to UTs and former French territory.

**DRDO Relevance:** Legal framework.
